01 The Integrated Model

Fifteen moving parts.
One accountable partner.

Most projects stall in the gaps — between the architect, the contractor, the supplier, the designer, and the approvals office. Delta Structures closes those gaps by owning the entire chain under one roof.

One point of contactOne number to call for design, execution, materials and paperwork.
Zero handoff gapsNo "that's the other contractor's problem." Every trade answers to the same project head.
End-to-end accountabilityFrom soil test to handover — and retrofitting support after.
WORKFLOW COMPRESSION
30+INTERFACE
15YOUR CONTACTS
0ACCOUNTABLE
PROJECT INTEGRATION & MGMT
30+ COORDINATION INTERFACESMultiple handoffs · Miscommunication · Delays · Diffused responsibility
COMPRESS 0%

Fifteen specialists. Thirty-plus ways for a message to die in transit — and you field all of it.

06 Meet the Founder

Engineering-led.
By design.

Delta Structures was founded on a simple observation: most project pain comes from coordination failure between separate contractors — not from the construction itself. So we built a firm where that coordination problem can't exist.

B.Tech. (Civil Engg.) — SRM University, Chennai PG – Advanced Construction Management — NICMAR, Pune Project Planning & Control — IIT Madras  (certificate) Financial Markets (Hons.) — Yale University  (certificate)

DS-KB-02 The Guide Library

Every domain, decoded.

These guides describe standard Indian construction practice and field checks aligned with IS codes (e.g. IS 456 concrete, IS 383 aggregates, IS 1893 seismic zoning, IS 303/710 plywood). They're general education — every building is unique, so treat anything structural as a reason for an on-site professional assessment, not a DIY diagnosis.

TOOL-01 Interactive Guide

The Approvals & Sanction Navigator

Before a single footing is dug, a paper trail has to clear. Tell us where and what you're building, and see the West Bengal sanction sequence laid out in order.

WB · Kolkata
1 · Local authority
2 · Building height
3 · Land use
Sanction first, then sell the dream

Foundation work on an unsanctioned plan risks demolition and penalties, and blocks bank loans and later mutation. The paperwork is slow — start it early, in parallel with design.

Your sanction path

KMC · Residential · up to G+3

TOOL-02 Interactive Guide

The Seasonal Construction Advisor

Kolkata's calendar quietly rules the site — the monsoon, the summer heat, the ideal winter window. Slide through the year and see which activity is ideal, which needs care, and which to avoid.

Kolkata climate
MonthSlide through the year
JanuaryWinter · prime window
Plan the wet trades around the rain

The monsoon isn't only lost days — excavation floods, concrete gets rained on, and paint won't cure. Winter (Nov–Feb) is Kolkata's best all-round build window.

January · Ideal for most work

Prime construction window

    Activity suitability by monthCurrent month highlighted
    IdealWorkable with careAvoid / high risk
    TOOL-03 Interactive Guide

    The Crack Decoder

    Structural crack or harmless shrinkage crack? Use the width gauge and pattern cards to read what your wall is telling you.

    Most-read guide
    Crack widthSlide to match yours
    0.5mm — roughly: hairline ≈ credit card edge, 3mm ≈ a matchstick
    Is it still moving? The pencil test

    Draw a fine pencil line across the crack and write today's date beside it. Re-check monthly. If the line breaks or the crack outgrows the mark, the crack is "live" — that changes the answer from "watch it" to "get it assessed", whatever the width.

    Hairline · Usually cosmetic

    Very likely plaster shrinkage

    At under 1mm, this is almost always the plaster or putty layer shrinking as it dries — common in the first year of any new wall. Cosmetic filling during the next repaint is enough. Note it, don't lose sleep over it.

    Now match the pattern & directionTap the closest match
    Pick a pattern aboveNo pattern selected

    Width tells you severity; the pattern and direction tell you the likely cause. Together they give a fairly reliable first read — the same one an engineer starts from.

    Side-by-side: shrinkage vs structural
    Shrinkage / plaster crackStructural crack
    CausePlaster or concrete drying and shrinking — a surface eventMovement: settlement, overload, corroding steel, thermal push
    WidthHairline, usually under 1mm, even along its lengthOften 3mm+, tapering (wider at one end), or growing over time
    ShapeRandom map/craze web, or straight along plaster jointsDiagonal ~45° from openings, long horizontals, steps in brick joints
    DepthSkin-deep — only in the plaster layerPasses through the wall; often visible on both sides
    Company it keepsAppears alone, typically in the first yearJamming doors/windows, sloping floors, gaps at wall–ceiling joints
    FixFill with crack filler / putty at next repaintEngineer assessment first — treatment depends on the cause
    The 30-second rule of thumb

    Thin + random + surface-only = relax. Wide + directional + through-the-wall = act. And any crack near a column, beam, or with rust stains bleeding from it skips the queue — that's exposed reinforcement corroding, which never improves on its own.

    Book a free crack assessment
    TOOL-04 Interactive Guide

    The Concrete Mix Explorer

    Pick a grade, then slide the water. Watch the same mix trade strength for workability — the one decision that quietly decides whether your M25 stays M25.

    New tool
    1 · Concrete gradeTap one
    CementSandCoarse Aggregate
    1 : 1.5 : 3RCC for homes, mild exposure
    2 · Water–cement ratioSlide it
    0.50litres water per kg cement
    Max w/c — Mild 0.55Moderate 0.50 · Severe 0.45
    The structural sweet spot

    Strong, durable, still workable

    As-placed strength
    ≈ 20 MPa
    Workability (slump)
    ≈ 40 mm
    Nominal mixes, decodedIS 456 : 2000
    GradeNominal ratio28-day strengthWhere it's used
    M101 : 3 : 610 MPaLevelling course / PCC — not structural
    M151 : 2 : 415 MPaPlain concrete, floor bases — not structural
    M201 : 1.5 : 320 MPaRCC for homes in mild exposure
    M25Design mix25 MPaKolkata structural default (moderate/severe)
    M30+Design mix30 MPa+High loads, coastal exposure, water-retaining
    The water trap

    Every extra litre of water buys easy placing and sells strength. Site crews add water to make concrete flow — that's how a specified M25 lands as M18. Ask for a plasticiser, not a bucket, then read the curing guide next.

    Talk RCC & concrete quality
    TOOL-05 Interactive Guide

    The Waterproofing Selector

    There's no single "waterproofing" — the right system depends on the surface and whether you're building fresh or chasing a leak. Pick both, and see what actually belongs there.

    New tool
    1 · Which surface?Tap the closest
    2 · What's the situation?Tap one
    The golden rule

    Waterproofing is a system, not a product — surface prep, the right membrane, sealed laps and a protection layer, in that order. Skip the prep and the best chemical on the market still peels.

    Bathroom · Building fresh

    2-component cementitious coating

      The systems, decodedSystems & service life
      SystemBest forIndicative life
      Cementitious coating (2-part)Bathrooms, sunken slabs, tanks, small terracesDecades under tiles
      Liquid membrane (PU / acrylic)Terraces, balconies, planters, complex shapes7–10 yrs exposed
      Bituminous membrane (torch-on)Large terraces, roofs, podiums8–12 yrs
      Integral crystalline / admixtureBasements, tanks, retaining wallsLife of the concrete
      Injection grouting (PU / acrylic)Chronic leaks, cracks, construction jointsTargeted repair
      Elastomeric exterior coatingExternal walls & facades5–8 yrs
      Before you tile or plaster

      Pond-test every wet area for 48 hours before anything covers the membrane. A leak found on bare concrete costs a morning; the same leak found after tiling costs the whole room.

      Get a waterproofing plan
      TOOL-06 Interactive Guide

      The Curing Clock

      Concrete earns strength on a curve, not a calendar. Slide to your slab's age to see roughly how much of its design strength is in — and what's safe to do at that stage.

      New tool
      Days since castingTypical OPC mix, properly cured
      7days — about 65% of design strength
      DAY 17142128 · 100%
      Why the daily watering matters

      Concrete hardens by a chemical reaction that consumes water. Strength skipped in the first two weeks is never recovered — curing is the cheapest strength you'll ever buy.

      Days 3–6 · Gaining fast

      Strength is being earned right now

        TOOL-07 Interactive Guide

        The Seismic Readiness Read

        Kolkata sits in Seismic Zone III on soft alluvial soil. Answer five quick questions about your building for a first read on where it stands — and what strengthening usually helps.

        New tool
        1 · When was it built?
        2 · How tall?
        3 · The ground floor is…
        4 · Visible distress?
        5 · The site sits on…
        Zone III · Low attention

        A reassuring baseline

          What actually helpsScoped after a structural audit
          MeasureWhat it doesTypically used when
          Column & beam jacketingWraps existing members in extra concrete or steel to add capacityUnder-sized or distressed frames
          Shear walls / steel bracingAdds stiffness where it's missing — the classic soft-storey fixOpen stilt / parking ground floors
          Carbon-fibre (FRP) wrapHigh-strength jacket where space or downtime rules out concreteLocalised strengthening, fast turnaround
          Foundation strengtheningUnderpinning or enlarging footings on weak or soft soilSettlement, filled or marshy sites
          Non-structural fixesAnchoring tanks, parapets, cladding & heavy fixturesCheap wins on almost every building
          Zone III is moderate, not zero

          Retrofitting is a seatbelt, not a bunker — it doesn't shrink the earthquake, it changes what happens to you in one. The open-parking soft storey is the single most common Kolkata vulnerability.

          Book a structural assessment
          TOOL-08 Interactive Guide

          The Foundation Type Matcher

          Soil, load and water decide the footing — not habit. Answer three questions and watch the foundation redraw itself, with the reasoning an engineer would give.

          Decision guide
          1Soil at footing depth
          2Building load
          3Water table
          4Column spacing / site
          Nothing replaces a soil test

          This is a first read. A geotechnical (SBC) report — boreholes, SPT and safe bearing capacity — is what a real footing design is built on, especially on Kolkata's alluvial soil.

          Best match

          Isolated footings

          Confidence86%

          Why it fits
            Watch out for
              Alternate optionStrip footings
              Avoid hereRaft
              Section through the ground — redraws with your answers
              TOOL-09 Interactive Guide

              The Damp & Seepage Diagnoser

              The same stain has very different causes depending on where it shows. Point to the damp on the house, and get the likely source and the fix that actually addresses it.

              Decision guide
              1 · Where is the damp? — tap a marker
              2 · When is it worst?
              Likely cause

              Confidence

              The right fix
                ×The wrong fix:
                Also worth ruling out:
                Treat the source, not the stain

                "Damp-proof" paint over a wet wall hides it for one season. Water always wins unless the path it travels is cut off at the source.

                TOOL-10 Interactive Guide

                The Concrete Grade Explorer

                M10 to M40 isn't a quality ladder — it's a map of where each grade belongs. Slide through the grades and watch which parts of a building light up, and the minimum the code demands for your exposure.

                M2525 MPa characteristic

                Min grade (RCC)M20

                Strength
                Durability

                Target mean strength31.6 MPa

                Where M25 belongsvs · element minimums
                TOOL-11 Interactive Guide

                The Site Readiness Gate

                The costliest mistakes get locked in the moment before a pour or a handover. Pick the stage, tick what's actually done, and the gate stays shut until every critical check is cleared.

                Quality gate
                Which gate?Tap one
                Gate closed

                Not ready yet

                Gate locked. Clear the critical checks to release it.
                TOOL-12 Interactive Guide

                The Load-Bearing Wall Identifier

                Before you knock a wall through for that open-plan look, find out what it's holding up. Answer four questions for a first read on whether it's safe to remove — or a job for an engineer first.

                Renovation safety
                1Wall thickness
                2Where is it?
                3What sits directly above?
                4Building system
                When in doubt, prop and ask

                This is guidance, not a permit to demolish. Removing a load path without a replacement beam is how floors sag and crack — an engineer’s visit costs less than one repair.

                First read

                Low risk
                Confidence

                What your answers say
                  Load path through the section
                  Quick visual checks
                  • Cracks above door jambs or along the ceiling line — movement above the wall.
                  • Beams or joists seated on, or running parallel into, the wall.
                  • The same wall aligned floor-to-floor and with footings below (check plans).
                  • Tap test: a dull, solid sound suggests full masonry, not a hollow partition.
                  Before you remove any wall
                  1. Full site inspection + architectural & structural drawings.
                  2. Confirm the load path and structural system.
                  3. Engage a structural engineer if anything is uncertain.
                  4. Design and install temporary props first.
                  5. Install the replacement beam / lintel if required.
                  6. Remove in stages, then monitor for movement.
                  TOOL-13 Interactive Guide

                  The Brick Bond Explorer

                  A wall's strength lives in its overlap. Switch bonds and watch the coursing rebuild — where the laps fall, where the closers go, and which bond your wall actually needs.

                  IS 1077 · SP 20
                  1 · Pick a bondWall rebuilds below
                  Wall elevation — face view
                  Wall thickness
                  Bricks per m²
                  Relative strength
                  Best for

                  TOOL-14 Interactive Simulation

                  The Beam Stress Heatmap

                  A simply supported RCC beam, live. Move the load and span and watch bending and shear repaint the beam — then switch on X-ray to see why the steel goes where it goes.

                  IS 456 · LIVE MODEL
                  1 · Load case
                  2 · Span6.0 m
                  3 · Load30 kN/m
                  4 · What to paint
                  Max moment
                  Max shear
                  Steel needed
                  Midspan deflection
                  Bending stress along the beamHover the beam for values at x
                  Indicative, not a design

                  Numbers assume a 300 × 500 beam, M25 · Fe500, d ≈ 450 mm. Real design adds load factors, deflection checks and detailing per IS 456 — this shows where the demand lives, which is the part worth understanding.

                  TOOL 13UNDER DEVELOPMENT

                  Steel & BBS Estimator

                  Turn a bar-bending schedule into tonnage, cutting wastage and a live steel cost — the line item that quietly decides an RCC budget.

                  TOOL 14UNDER DEVELOPMENT

                  Staircase Designer

                  Feed in the floor-to-floor height and get a code-compliant riser, tread, going and headroom layout — no more awkward last step.

                  TOOL 15UNDER DEVELOPMENT

                  Slab Span & Depth Checker

                  Enter a span and its supports; the IS 456 span-to-depth ratio returns the slab thickness that won’t deflect or crack.

                  TOOL 16UNDER DEVELOPMENT

                  Brick & Mortar Calculator

                  Wall dimensions in, brick count with cement and sand out — including the wastage a real Kolkata site actually orders.

                  TOOL 17UNDER DEVELOPMENT

                  Rainwater Harvesting Sizer

                  Roof area against Kolkata’s ~1,600 mm annual rainfall sizes your storage tank and recharge pit — the rebate-ready numbers.

                  TOOL 18UNDER DEVELOPMENT

                  Cost-per-Sq-Ft Benchmarker

                  Compare your build’s ₹/sq ft against current Kolkata norms by finish level — spot padding before you sign.

                  HomeAbout

                  One firm. One head. One line of accountability.

                  Delta Structures exists to end the "five contractors" problem — the architect blaming the contractor, the contractor blaming the supplier, and you stuck refereeing between them.

                  Delta StructuresSingle point of contactKolkata
                  01 The Story

                  Why Delta Structures exists

                  Ask anyone who has built or renovated in Kolkata what hurt most, and it’s rarely the concrete. It’s the coordination — the architect blaming the contractor, the contractor blaming the supplier, and the owner stuck refereeing between them.

                  Delta Structures was founded to remove that referee job entirely. Planning, building, upgrading and managing sit under one roof, one project head, one line of accountability. We handle the complexities. You see the progress.

                  Accountable

                  One team answers for every trade on your site.

                  Transparent

                  Realistic timelines across every specialist, milestone photos and updates at each step — you're kept informed throughout.

                  Educating

                  An informed client is our best client — hence the Knowledge Hub.

                  02 The Founder
                  NameRaj Sekhar Ghosh
                  RoleFounder · Civil Engineer

                  Credentials, chosen deliberately

                  Four qualifications — one for each discipline the firm runs on.

                  DisciplineQualificationInstitution
                  FoundationB.Tech. (Civil Engineering)SRM University, Chennai
                  Construction depthPG — Advanced Construction ManagementNICMAR, Pune
                  Delivery disciplineProject Planning & Control  (certificate)IIT Madras
                  Financial rigourFinancial Markets (Hons.)  (certificate)Yale University

                  That combination — engineering, construction management, planning and finance — is deliberate. Projects fail on coordination and cashflow as often as on concrete; Delta Structures is built to manage all three.

                  HomeLegal

                  Legal

                  HomeGet a Free Quote

                  Your free quote, scoped properly.

                  Five short steps. The preliminary estimate is free — a ballpark scope and indicative cost range. If you want engineer-verified figures, an optional Detailed QTO & BOQ is available — pricing is project-specific and shared with you on request — fully credited against your bill if you build with us.

                  Step 1 of 5 · Select servicesDraft saved on this device

                  Which domains does your project touch?

                  Select as many as apply — most projects combine two or three. Search across all 148+ sub-services, or browse the domains below.

                  Tell us about the project

                  Rough numbers are fine — this shapes the preliminary estimate.

                  Optional — each answer below helps us prepare a sharper preliminary estimate.

                  Drawings, a sanction plan or a few site photos help us estimate faster — but they’re optional. Files stay on your device — on submit they’re sent to us through WhatsApp (attached automatically where your device supports it, otherwise you’ll attach them in the chat).

                  How do we reach you?

                  Used only to send your estimate and coordinate a site discussion.

                  Optional add-on: Detailed QTO & BOQ

                  Entirely optional — your free preliminary estimate happens either way.

                  Engineer-prepared QTO & BOQ QTO (Quantity Takeoff): itemised material & labour quantities calculated from drawings and site data. BOQ (Bill of Quantities): the formal itemised costing document — used for accurate budgeting, bank loans, or comparing contractors line by line.

                  Precise, itemised figures on Delta Structures letterhead — a professional document you can take to your bank or compare against any other contractor's quote.

                  Priced on requestProject-specific quote · adjustable against project cost
                  How the fee works: pricing is project-specific — quoted for your project on request and confirmed in writing before any work begins.
                  This isn't an extra cost if you build with us: the full QTO & BOQ fee is credited against your final project bill when you proceed with Delta Structures. It's a credit against future work, not a cash-refund voucher — full policy in the FAQ.

                  Review & submit

                  Everything look right? Tap any "Edit" to jump back.

                  Free preliminary estimate · No payment on this path
